claim_bullets.bullet_text: JavaScript uses === to test if two values are identical, and !== to test if they are not identical (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.21-23).
Values of the same type with the same content are identical, such as strings, numbers, and booleans (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.21-23).
Arrays and functions are reference types; even if they look the same, they are not identical with === (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.21-23).

source_record_text: This chapter discusses JavaScript values, identity, and the === operator, using coffee cup metaphors to explain value types and reference types.
